stories.title: EU countries weigh fresh plan to salvage Russia sanctions package
stories.summary: BRUSSELS — EU ambassadors will on Thursday examine a fresh proposal on a sweeping new set of Russia sanctions after Greece again vetoed the measures over concerts for its fuel shipping industry.  The fresh proposal, put forward by EU rotating presidency holders Ireland, offers a credible way out of the deadlock that has held up progress on the package for weeks, two diplomats familiar with the negotiations told POLITICO. They were granted anonymity to discuss the confidential discussions. “

stories.title: Hungary: Ex-PM Orban pledges resistance to Magyar 'tyranny'
stories.summary: Hungary's former prime minister alleges that his successor has "dismantled the rule of law and eliminated checks and balances." Peter Magyar has pursued Viktor Orban and his allies since winning April's elections.

stories.title: My friends left Ukraine after the Russian invasion. Now I wonder if we live in the same world | Diana Berg
stories.summary: Anger, guilt, shame: the deepest unseen wound of this war is the rift between those who left and those who stayed behind “Can we reschedule?” Over the years of working with western colleagues, I had learned to accept that they don’t fully understand what war feels like from the inside. They would ask me to find someone in occupied Mariupol for an interview, or seem genuinely puzzled that I couldn’t just log on at a time that suited them. I made my peace with this long ago – they don’t have to un

stories.title: Puszta kézzel küzdött egy utas életéért egy magyar orvos a fedélzeten, a Ryanair szerint minden járaton van defibrillátor
stories.summary: Egy magyar kardiológus a kisgyerekével együtt utazott a légitársaság Madrid–Budapest járatán, amikor egy férfi rosszul lett. Az utast egy idő után újra kellett éleszteni. A légiutas-kísérők azt mondták az orvosnak, hogy semmilyen orvosi műszer nincs a fedélzeten. A Ryanair viszont azt írta a Telexnek, hogy minden gépükön van defibrillátor, de azt nem kérte az orvos.

stories.title: Trump threatens retaliatory strikes on key Iranian infrastructure as conflict escalates
stories.summary: President Donald Trump on Wednesday warned that the United States would strike Iranian bridges and power plants if Iran continues to attack commercial ships passing through the Strait of Hormuz. In  a post on Truth Social , Trump proclaimed that any type of Iranian attack on vessels traveling through the waterway would trigger corresponding American strikes on key infrastructure, including locations in or around Tehran. “From this point forward, any time the Islamic Repu

stories.title: Trump administration steers $5B toward AI research
stories.summary: Federal agencies have committed more than $5 billion to an Energy Department-led effort to use artificial intelligence to accelerate scientific research, White House Office of Science and Technology Policy Director Michael Kratsios announced Wednesday. stories.title: Trump’s air war in Iran may be reaching its limits
stories.summary: President Donald Trump’s latest threat to bomb Iranian infrastructure is unlikely to rattle Tehran — and instead underscores the limits of what an air war alone can accomplish. The Trump administration has become increasingly frustrated by Iran’s refusal to capitulate, as the two sides have escalated attacks over the past month in a fight for control of the Strait of Hormuz, a key waterway for world energy supplies. Now, Trump has pledged to hit “one bridge or power plant” for eve

stories.summary: The Trump administration announced a major civil nuclear deal with Saudi Arabia on Wednesday, after decades of on-and-off conversations between the country and the Obama, Biden and Trump White Houses. The Department of Energy confirmed in a press release that Energy Secretary Chris Wright and Saudi Minister of Energy Prince Abdulaziz bin Salman signed two agreements that would allow private American nuclear companies to export to the Saudi market. stories.title: OpenAI’s models broke free and launched a cyberattack. US Congress wants new rules before it happens again.

stories.title: Zelenskyy under growing pressure to reinstate sacked defence minister
stories.summary: Campaigners in Ukraine pledge to continue nationwide protests until Mykhailo Fedorov gets his job back Volodymyr Zelenskyy was under growing pressure to reinstate his sacked defence minister on Wednesday, after campaigners said they would continue indefinite nationwide protests until Mykhailo Fedorov got his job back. stories.title: France records more than 5,700 excess deaths during June heatwave

stories.title: Mamdani urges US government to arrest Netanyahu if Israeli PM visits US
stories.summary: New York mayor admits he does not have authority to arrest Netanyahu, adding Israeli PM is ‘architect of a horrific genocide’ Zohran Mamdani , the New York City mayor, acknowledged on Tuesday he does not have the authority to arrest Benjamin Netanyahu , but urged US federal authorities to do so if the Israeli prime minister visits the US later this year. “Benjamin Netanyahu is a war criminal, the architect of a horrific genocide against the Palestinian people,” Mamdani said in a video posted to

stories.title: Michelin-starred restaurant owner in South Korea faces jail over ant dessert
stories.summary: Prosecutors seek a one-year sentence and heavy fine after chef garnished dish with insects not approved for consumption Prosecutors in South Korea have asked a court to sentence the owner of a Michelin-starred restaurant to one year in prison for serving a dessert topped with ants, an insect not approved for consumption under local food safety law. The request, made on Monday, also sought a 20m won (£10,108) fine for the company that runs the restaurant, Yonhap news agency reported . Continue re

stories.title: Australia to warn China it won’t be bullied by ‘provocative‘ actions as it builds military and nuclear arsenal
stories.summary: Foreign minister Penny Wong will deliver speech criticising China’s recent launch of a nuclear-capable submarine-launched missile Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Penny Wong will warn China that Australia and other neighbouring nations will not be bullied by its “provocative” actions, after its involvement in another “destabilising and dangerous” incident this week. The foreign affairs minister – who will deliver the address on Thursday in the Philippines, where the 5

stories.title: British woman jailed in Hong Kong for blackmail after accusing banker of rape
stories.summary: Isabel Rose, 26, sentenced to six years for blackmail and perverting course of public justice over her allegations against British man A British woman who reported being raped to police in Hong Kong has been sentenced to six years in prison for blackmail and perverting the course of public justice. Isabel Rose, 26, from south-east London, travelled to Hong Kong in early 2024 to visit a British banker she had met while travelling in south-east Asia. Continue reading...

stories.title: Germany's growing interest in Africa: A win for both sides?
stories.summary: Germany is deepening its footprint across Africa, as Foreign Minister Johann Wadephul embarks on his third trip to the continent in 2026. His top-level talks will focus on security, skills development and investment.

stories.title: Germany: Merz replaces key figure following surrogacy row
stories.summary: Thorsten Frei, a close confidant of German Chancellor Friedrich Merz, is taking over the role as parliamentary group leader for the conservative CDU/CSU bloc. The position is a powerful one.

stories.title: DW correspondent Alican Uludag released, ordered not to leave Turkey
stories.summary: Alican Uludag was freed Wednesday, a day after being taken back into custody on new charges. The arrest had come just two months after he was released pending trial in a separate case.

stories.title: How Russia is luring foreign nationals to fight in Ukraine
stories.summary: More and more, Russia is recruiting men from the world’s poorest countries to send them to the front in the Ukraine war. Why are people joining up?

stories.title: Who could be the next UN Secretary-General?
stories.summary: For the first time in a decade, the United Nations will soon choose a new Secretary-General. DW takes a look at the six candidates, what they stand for and the challenges they and the UN will face.

stories.title: Louvre's Apollo Gallery reopens, 9 months after heist
stories.summary: The 17th-century Apollo Gallery reopened to the public without precious jewels or other objects previously on display.

stories.title: US judge sets Maduro trial date for June 2027
stories.summary: US prosecutors accuse Maduro of using the power of the Venezuelan state to protect and promote illegal drug smuggling.

stories.title: Germany news: Prosecutors search Deutsche Bank headquarters
stories.summary: Officials are reportedly investigating fraudulent tax claims made by the bank's Postbank subsidiary between 2008 and 2010. Meanwhile, the story of Timmy the whale continues with new DNA results.

stories.title: France: Minister quits over pesticide bill — but is staying
stories.summary: Environment Minister Monique Barbut submitted her resignation in protest over the reintroduction of pesticides that scientists say harm human health as well as bees. But President Emmanuel Macron refused to accept it.

stories.title: British woman jailed for blackmail after accusing banker of rape in Hong Kong
stories.summary: Isabel Rose was convicted of trying to extort £100,000 from the UK banker and perverting the course of justice.

stories.title: Tankers make sharp U-turns after Houthi shipping threat
stories.summary: All of the ships were travelling to or from Saudi ports before changing course, ship-tracking data shows.

stories.title: Mysterious white foam draws crowds on Nigerian highway
stories.summary: The environmental agency has warned people to avoid going near the substance as it could pose a health risk.

stories.title: Wreckage of Pan Am plane that shaped aviation safety found 74 years on
stories.summary: The deaths of 52 people on the Clipper Endeavor led to the introduction of mandatory pre-flight safety briefings.

stories.title: Two Russians handed down jail terms for terrorism and spying in Angola
stories.summary: The two, a political consultant and a translator, denied allegations of fomenting protests last year.

stories.title: Watch: Louvre reopens gallery after jewellery heist
stories.summary: The Louvre Museum reopens its Apollo Gallery nine months after a robbery that shocked France.

stories.title: Modelling scout linked to Epstein found dead in Paris
stories.summary: Daniel Siad, whose name appears in the Epstein files thousands of times, denied knowledge of the threat Jeffrey Epstein posed.

stories.title: Police formally investigate woman after Brit stabbed to death in French village
stories.summary: Karen Carter was found with stab wounds in the Dordogne village she had lived in for over a decade.

stories.title: Congo's Ebola outbreak kills more than 1,000 people
stories.summary: The World Health Organization has said that the true scale of the outbreak could be two to four times larger than official figures indicate.

stories.title: Blocked by censors, China's animal lovers take fight against abuse offline and overseas
stories.summary: The killing of a dog and her puppies in Guangdong has sparked outrage inside and outside the country.

stories.title: A year after deadly jet crash at Bangladesh school, families demand answers
stories.summary: This week marks one year since a military jet struck a school in Dhaka, killing 36, most of them children.

stories.title: Trump threatens to target Iran's bridges and power plants if Hormuz attacks persist
stories.summary: Donald Trump says the US will respond any time Iranian forces shoot at a ship in the Strait of Hormuz.

stories.title: Watch: What we know about the US-Saudi nuclear deal
stories.summary: The BBC's Sarah Smith explains the implications of the landmark cooperation agreement between both countries.

stories.title: Russia's businesses under strain from Ukraine's attacks on Wildberries
stories.summary: Ukrainian drones have hit several retail warehouses belonging to Russia's biggest online retailer Wildberries in a matter of days.

stories.title: Even camels can't cope: Africa's ships of the desert hit by rising temperatures
stories.summary: Herders and experts tell the BBC how Africa's camels are suffering because of extreme heat.

stories.title: Canada's 'powerful' dairy sector is in Trump's trade crosshairs
stories.summary: Canada's dairy supply management system is in the spotlight after the US president singled it out as one of three main irritants used to justify new tariffs.

stories.title: US signs landmark nuclear deal with Saudi Arabia
stories.summary: The US Department of Energy says the "peaceful" co-operation agreement will give US firms "great access" to the Saudi nuclear energy programme.

stories.title: India's Modi government under growing pressure as 'cockroach' protests intensify
stories.summary: The CJP got a boost on Tuesday when the opposition Congress party demonstrated outside the PM's house.

stories.title: EU sieht sich von Trumps neuen Generika-Zöllen nicht betroffen
stories.summary: BRÜSSEL – Die Europäische Kommission erklärte am Mittwoch, sie gehe davon aus, dass europäische Generika von den jüngsten Zöllen des US-Präsidenten Donald Trump ausgenommen seien. Sie verwies darauf, dass sich Washington im Rahmen des transatlantischen Handelsabkommens vom vergangenen Jahr zu einem Zollsatz von null verpflichtet habe. Trump hatte am Dienstag angekündigt, ab August 2028 einen Zollsatz von 100 Prozent auf Generika zu erheben, um die Arzneimittelproduktion zurück in die Verei

stories.title: India’s Cockroach movement protesters show no sign of backing down
stories.summary: Thousands of people again on the streets of Delhi calling for education reforms despite police violence on Monday Taru had come to the protest prepared this time. In one hand was an anti-government poster and in the other was a bicycle helmet. “We need to have protection in case Delhi police beat us up again,” said the 25-year-old, who stood among her friends. “We are not scared of them and we won’t run away, no matter what they do. We’ll stay here till they listen.” On Tuesday, central Delhi st

stories.title: Drone strike sparks major fire in Sumy, northeastern Ukraine
stories.summary: A home improvement shopping centre in Sumy, Ukraine, catches fire after a reported Russian FPV drone strike as firefighters battle the blaze.

stories.title: Japan records its first ‘cruelly hot day’ as cities swelter in 40-degree heat
stories.summary: In April Japan coined new term kokushobi for days above 40C as country advises workers to wear shorts to the office to cope with stifling heat Japan logged its first official kokushobi or “cruelly hot day” on Tuesday since the new term for when temperatures top 40 degrees Celsius was introduced in April. The Japan Meteorological Agency coined the new designation to warn the public of the heat dangers of days that were once freak weather events but have now become more common. The central Japan c

stories.title: Sperm whales change ‘vowel’ sounds when boats are nearby, scientists discover
stories.summary: Researchers listening off the Caribbean island of Dominica found variations in the mammals’ audible clicks when marine traffic was close Sperm whales produce a specific pattern of clicking noises when they hear the noise of boats, researchers have found. The pattern is so distinct that the scientists who identified it said they can now use it to predict when ships are nearby. Researchers from Project Ceti (the Cetacean Translation Initiative), a non-profit research organisation working to decode

stories.title: Scorched earth and a hungry wasp: photos of the day – Wednesday
stories.summary: The Guardian’s picture editors select photographs from around the world Continue reading...

stories.title: Two trams collide on bridge in Rotterdam, injuring at least 15 people - video
stories.summary: Webcam footage shows the moment two trams collide on the Erasmus Bridge in the Dutch city. Officials said at least 15 people were injured, one seriously, in the collision that occurred on Wednesday Continue reading...

stories.title: Nicaragua’s congress announces ‘work plan’ to suspend elections
stories.summary: US secretary of state says international community should ‘join forces’ against authoritarian Ortega regime Nicaragua’s congress, controlled by the country’s long-serving president, Daniel Ortega, has announced a “work plan” to implement the authoritarian leader’s order that the country should no longer hold elections. The UN and the US strongly condemned Sunday’s announcement by Ortega, who has been in power since 2007 and, after a controversial constitutional change last year, now governs alon

stories.title: Model scout for Jeffrey Epstein found dead at his home near Paris
stories.summary: Daniel Siad faced allegations of rape and trafficking in France and appeared in more than 1,000 documents in Epstein files A modelling scout who recruited women for Jeffrey Epstein has been found dead at his home near Paris, French officials have said. Daniel Siad, 69, was under investigation by the prosecutors specialising in human trafficking in France, accused of rape and other charges. His name appeared in more than 1,000 documents in the US Department of Justice Epstein files that were decl

stories.title: Can VR help you achieve your goals?
stories.summary: A Max Planck researcher has developed VR and smartphone tools that let you "meet" your future self. The science behind it is more powerful than it sounds.

stories.title: Turkey-PKK talks: Is peace finally possible?
stories.summary: A year ago, the Kurdish militant group PKK announced an end to its armed struggle. Since then, peace talks with Turkey have progressed slowly, but some observers believe they could succeed.

stories.title: Police station opens in Hitler's birth house
stories.summary: Controversy has surrounded the renovation of the building in Braunau, Austria to this day. The debate points to something larger: the continued need to remember history and confront the Nazi past.

stories.title: Will surge in German business bankruptcies hit growth?
stories.summary: Company insolvencies in Germany are rising sharply. Is this a sign of deeper economic weakness, or just a market correction that could ultimately benefit future growth?

stories.title: Can India become the next semiconductor hub after Taiwan?
stories.summary: India is investing billions to build a domestic semiconductor industry as major industrial powers seek to diversify supply chains beyond Taiwan.

stories.title: Congo: Ebola deaths increase to over 1,000
stories.summary: The fatality rate of the latest Ebola virus outbreak since mid-May is far higher than in previous outbreaks. No vaccines or treatments exist for the new strain.

stories.title: What's holding up the Gaza stabilization mission?
stories.summary: A multinational stabilization mission is supposed to ensure the reconstruction of Gaza. Without political consensus among Israel, Hamas and the Arab states, its implementation remains uncertain.

stories.title: Iran war has cost US $37.5bn so far, Hegseth says, as Pentagon seeks billions more
stories.summary: The hearing was disrupted by protests and a shouting match as Hegseth testified at a pivotal time in the war.

stories.title: Trump says US has no interest in Iran talks as cost of war increases
stories.summary: The US entered its 11th consecutive night of strikes on Iran, while Tehran expanded its offensive against Gulf states. Meanwhile, Pakistan stepped up diplomatic efforts in hopes of salvaging the interim deal between the two.

stories.title: Outcry in Algeria over doctor deaths after gruelling on-call shifts
stories.summary: A resident neurosurgeon died of a heart attack after a 24-hour shift, and an anaesthesiologist died days later — deaths colleagues say reflect years of chronic overwork, understaffing and inadequate conditions in Algeria's public hospitals, demanding a revision of on-call rules dating back to 2013.

stories.title: Watch: Beluga whales travel by plane and lorry to US from closed Canadian marine park
stories.summary: Six beluga whales have arrived in Illinois and Texas after being moved from a Canadian marine park that could no longer care for them.

stories.title: Aerial footage reveals the scale of Spain’s La Mierla wildfire
stories.summary: Aerial footage shows the scale of the La Mierla wildfire in Guadalajara, Spain, as firefighters battle a blaze that has burned up to 30,000 hectares.

stories.title: Mamdani says NYC lacks authority to arrest Israeli PM Netanyahu
stories.summary: Netanyahu's office has dubbed the ICC a "kangaroo court" and called the arrest warrant against the Israeli leader "bogus."

stories.title: Zendaya's ancient Iranian earrings stir debate over looted artefacts
stories.summary: Experts said the Iranian 3,000-year-old gold disc medallions Zendaya wore at a promo event for Nolan's Odyssey were "likely looted" and constituted "neocolonialism". Separately, a Zoroastrian symbol worn by IShowSpeed at the World Cup final drew its own debate about Persian cultural identity.

stories.title: 'It will get worse': World’s second-coldest capital faces record heat
stories.summary: Experts warn that the extreme heat reflects a broader climate trend that is increasing health, water and food security risks across Central Asia.

stories.title: Police station opens at Hitler's birthplace in bid to rid site of Nazi link
stories.summary: The opening follows years of controversy about what to do with the 17th-Century former inn, where the Nazi dictator was born.

stories.title: Drones hit Wildberries warehouses in Russia for second week running
stories.summary: Fires broke out at Wildberries warehouse sites in Krasnodar and Stavropol after overnight drone attacks, injuring 15 people and forcing both facilities to suspend operations.

stories.title: Elutasították, hogy nyilvántartásba vegyék az uniós aláírásgyűjtést a „nem nyugati” bevándorlás moratóriumára
stories.summary: Az Európai Bizottság szerint a javaslat diszkriminatív és uniós alapértékeket sért. A testület átengedte az online korellenőrzés elleni polgári kezdeményezést.

stories.title: Csótányoknak nevezik magukat az indiai diákok, brutálisan leverték a tüntetésüket
stories.summary: Akár veszélyt is jelenthetnek Narendra Modi kormányára az indiaiak kétfarkú kutyái. Az ellenzéki pártok is felszálltak a hullámra, egy vezetőjüket már be is vitték a rendőrök.

stories.title: A KDNP-s politikus szerint a templomba járó szavazók fele inkább a Tiszára szavazott áprilisban
stories.summary: Latorcai Csaba, a KDNP alelnöke azt mondta Tusványoson, hogy már a 2024-es EP-választáson látták, hogy a keresztény szavazók jelentős része elfordult tőlük, a Fideszt azonban ez nem érdekelte. Zárug Péter Farkas politológus szerint a Fidesznek vége, a KDNP-nek még lehet jövője.

stories.title: Az ország 89 százaléka közepesen vagy súlyosan aszályos
stories.summary: A nagy termőterületű vármegyékben még rosszabb a helyzet.

stories.summary: A kormány előterjesztése szerint akár három év börtönbüntetést és 45 ezer eurós pénzbírságot is kaphatnak majd azok, akik megsértik a szavazás tisztaságát.

stories.title: Hogy került hirtelen képbe pont a 3-as metró meghosszabbítása, és miért ilyen kurtán?
stories.summary: Megnéztük, hogy miért nem a 2-es vagy a 4-as metrót rántották elő a kalapból, és hogy akkor már miért nem viszik a 3-ast végig Káposztásmegyerig, ahogy korábban tervezték.

stories.title: Hivatali visszaélés miatt nyomoz a rendőrség a Neptun, a Kréta és a Poszeidon ügyében is
stories.summary: A rendszerek azoknak a cégeknek a terméke, amelyek Fauszt Zoltánhoz, Palkovics László egykori miniszter barátjához köthetők. Tanács Zoltán nemrég jelentette be, hogy feljelentést tettek ezek miatt.

stories.title: Komoly vitákat generál Japánban, hogy a nagy meleg miatt a férfiak elkezdtek rövidnadrágban dolgozni
stories.summary: Sok nő igazságtalannak érzi, hogy a melegben nekik még mindig harisnyát kell hordani. Mások viszont attól tartanak, hogy kritika éri őket a lábszőrük miatt.

stories.title: Jönnek pécére az eredeti, klasszikus Xbox-játékok
stories.summary: Most még csak négy, de ezek a Game Passba is azonnal bekerülnek.

stories.title: Rendőrőrs nyílt Adolf Hitler szülőházában
stories.summary: Azt remélik, így kevesebb neonáci fog majd a helyszínre zarándokolni.

stories.title: Két hét alatt közel 7 milliárdot spórolt meg a közmédia új vezetése
stories.summary: Az MTVA közleménye szerint már most érzékelhető a nézőszám javulása, miközben folyamatosan zajlanak a vizsgálatok, többek között a kerékpáros közvetítési jogok esetében is.

stories.summary: Az évek óta az MTVA tiltólistáján szereplő refpper a közrádió stúdiójából posztolt szelfit.

stories.summary: A Galaxy Z Fold8 Ultra legbikább verziójáért ennyit kell fizetni, de cserébe tényleg csúcskategóriás mobilt kapunk. Kipróbáltuk pár héttel a megjelenés előtt.

stories.summary: Vozinha annak ellenére került be, hogy hivatalosan a spanyolok kapusát választották meg a torna legjobbjának.

stories.title: Németországban már nem csak bűnelkövető afgánokat toloncolhatnak ki
stories.summary: Több kritika is érte Németországot, amiért együttműködnek a tálibokkal, de a kancellár elutasítja ezeket.

stories.title: Zelenszkij alulmaradt menesztett miniszterével szemben, aki tovább szkanderezik az elnökkel
stories.summary: A népszerű védelmi miniszter múlt heti leváltása után kirobbant tüntetések hatására az ukrán elnök megvált a hadsereg főparancsnokától és a vezérkari főnöktől is, éppen úgy, ahogyan azt Mihajlo Fedorov követelte. És még nincs vége: Fedorov egyelőre nem fogadta el a Zelenszkij által felkínált új pozíciókat.

stories.title: Germany news: Merz hosts Azerbaijan President Aliyev
stories.summary: Talks between the German chancellor and Ilham Aliyev have focused on energy. Meanwhile, Interior Minister Alexander Dobrindt will present a report on sexual offenses against children.

stories.title: Germany records rise in sexual violence against children
stories.summary: Germany's Criminal Police Office has reported over 17,000 cases of sexual violence against children in the past year. Organizations such as Zartbitter provide support to victims.

stories.title: Russia says bus strike kills 5 in border region with Ukraine
stories.summary: The strike comes as Ukraine launched more than hundreds of drones at Moscow after a Russian missile barrage on Kyiv, as President Zelenskyy sought to calm growing tensions over Ukraine’s military leadership.

stories.title: Tanker attacked in Strait of Hormuz as US strikes Iran for tenth consecutive night
stories.summary: As fighting escalates between both sides, Iran's interior minister travelled to Pakistan, a key mediator in the conflict, for talks.

stories.title: Iran's IRGC claims attack on Amazon's main data hub in Bahrain
stories.summary: The IRGC claimed its cruise missiles destroyed Amazon's central data infrastructure in Bahrain and hit US air defence systems. Bahraini, US and Amazon officials have not responded.

stories.title: Lebanese army begins deployment to southern regions after Israel deal
stories.summary: Beirut announced the beginning of its mobilisation to the southern Lebanese regions as President Aoun prepares for a high-stakes meeting with US President Donald Trump on Tuesday to conclude his four-day visit to Washington.

stories.title: UN reports 144 dead or missing after migrant boat is stranded at sea for 25 days
stories.summary: Two children onboard the boat bound for the Canary Islands lost their entire family before the passengers were picked up by a rescue operation from Mauritania.

stories.title: Trump promises US support for Lebanon in White House meeting with president
stories.summary: Lebanon and Israel have held rare direct talks mediated by Washington. Aoun’s visit was the first to the White House by a Lebanese president since 2009.

stories.title: Australia spent more than $9m for each asylum seeker held on Nauru last year – so why can’t they afford to buy food?

A huge crowd, largely made up of students and gen Z-ers, filled the streets in the heart of India’s capital from early Monday morning shouting anti-government slogans. Hundreds had already

stories.title: Healthy diet too expensive for one in three people globally, UN report finds
stories.summary: Global hunger declined for the third year in a row in 2025 but experts call for action to bring down costs of fruit, vegetables and dairy as report shows 2.69bn people can’t afford to eat well One in three people cannot afford to eat a healthy diet, a new report has found, prompting calls for urgent action on food affordability. There are 2.69 billion people unable to afford a diverse diet that meets their energy and nutrient needs, and it will take “immense” amounts of work to achieve the globa

stories.title: Rescuers in Afghanistan search for missing and dead after flash floods destroy homes
stories.summary: National disaster agency confirms ‘severe financial and human losses’ in eastern province of Nuristan Rescue crews in Afghanistan are searching for survivors and bodies among the rubble of houses destroyed by devastating flash floods that authorities said had killed nearly two dozen people and left more than 100 people missing. The Afghanistan national disaster management authority said on Tuesday that 23 people had died, 80 had been injured and more than 100 were missing after floods struck the

stories.title: What the EU wants from Andy Burnham
stories.summary: European leaders have made positive noises about the new UK PM, but he is likely to face the same restrictions as Starmer Andy Burnham is unknown in Brussels, but that will soon change. In his schedule for the autumn is an EU-UK summit that may underscore the same dilemmas that have confronted previous British prime ministers in the relationship with Europe. EU leaders were quick to offer congratulations to Britain’s prime minister, the seventh since the 2016 Brexit referendum. The European Comm

stories.title: Health worker monitored for Ebola in London hospital after working in DRC
stories.summary: UK resident was evacuated from Democratic Republic of the Congo where there is an outbreak of the disease A humanitarian worker is being monitored at a specialist hospital in London after potential exposure to the Ebola virus. The UK resident was medically evacuated from the Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC) where the worker had been treating patients with the disease, the UK Health Security Agency (UKHSA) said. Continue reading...

stories.title: Trump announces new tariffs on generic drugs to take effect in 2028 – as it happened

According to the American Automobile Association (AAA), a gallon of fuel is now just over $4 –  almost a dollar more than a year ago. It comes as Brent Crude , the international oil benchmark, is now up by almost 2% to $90.92 a barrel . Continue reading...

stories.title: Kaptál legalább egy „köszönöm”-öt? – kőkemény interjúban szembesítették Hankó Balázst az NKA-üggyel

stories.title: Újabb rendkívüli parlamenti ülések összehívását kezdeményezte Ruff Bálint
stories.summary: Július 27-én és 28-án is ülésezhet az Országgyűlés. Tárgyalhatnak többek között a mezőgazdaságról és a Nemzeti Vagyonvisszaszerzési és Vagyonvédelmi Hivatalról.

stories.title: Gajdos Lászlóék nyomoznak a hatvanpusztai zebrák ügyében
stories.summary: Kiderült hány zebra fordult meg Hatvanpusztán 2024 és 2026 között.

stories.title: Lemondott a legfőbb ügyész
stories.summary: „Nem kívánok tovább részese lenni annak, hogy a személyem körül gerjesztett, valótlan alapokon álló, politikai célt szolgáló konfliktus az intézményi bizalom további erodálásának eszközévé váljon” – indokolta a lemondást Nagy Gábor Bálint. A lemondást Forsthoffer Ágnes ideiglenes államfőn keresztül az Országgyűlés elnöki feladatait ellátó alelnökhöz nyújtotta be.

stories.title: Nyár végén már dönthet is a parlament a Független Közmédia Testület tagjairól
stories.summary: A Független Közmédia Testületbe három tagot a kormánypárt, hármat az ellenzék, hármat pedig médiaszakmai szervezetek jelölhetnek – mondta Radnai Márk a parlament művelődési bizottságának ülésén.

stories.title: Trump 100 százalékos vámot vetett ki az importált generikus gyógyszerekre, ami később 200-ra emelkedik majd
stories.summary: De két évig még marad a 0 százalékos vám az Egyesült Államokba behozott generikus gyógyszereknél.

stories.title: A NAV-tól igazolt új vezetőt a Kormányzati Ellenőrzési Hivatal
stories.summary: Szeverényi Dávid közgazdász, és főosztályvezetőként irányította az együttműködést az Európai Csalás Elleni Hivatallal.

stories.title: Lemondott a hegyvidéki alpolgármester, szerinte Kovács Gergely autoriter módon vezeti a kerületet
stories.summary: A momentumos Vadász Gábor kemény állításokat fogalmazott meg a hegyvidéki polgármesterről. Kovács Gergely örül a lemondásának, mert szerinte Vadász akadályozta a hivatal működését.

stories.title: NKA-botrány: újabb személyt vettek őrizetbe és hallgattak ki gyanúsítottként
stories.summary: A Fidesz-frakció szerint „egy újabb volt kormánytisztviselőt, a Fidesz-frakció munkatársát vittek el otthonából”. Információink szerint a Fidesz szervereit azért foglalták le, mert nem működtek együtt a nyomozókkal az adatok átadásakor.

stories.title: How heat waves are messing with your sleep
stories.summary: Residents across Europe have struggled to sleep during the latest record heat waves. But as the planet continues to warm, sleep deprivation could create an epidemic of mental and physical ill-health.

stories.title: Why the youth jobs crisis is becoming global
stories.summary: Young people in much of the world face growing competition for entry-level jobs as graduate numbers rise, hiring slows and AI-driven disruption adds uncertainty to career prospects.

stories.title: Is Germany investing in the right weapons?
stories.summary: Germany wants to build up its army and pour hundreds of millions of euros into its defenses. But in a world where the battlefield is increasingly dominated by cheap drones, how many expensive tanks does the country need?

stories.title: 'Antifa' song climbs to top of charts after TV show cancellation
stories.summary: German rapper Danger Dan and pianist Igor Levitt had been invited to play a song for the 100th episode of a political satire show on TV. The performance was canceled at the last minute due to its message.

stories.title: Trump imposes 50% tariffs on Canadian goods citing disputes over autos
stories.summary: President Donald Trump on Monday imposed 50% tariffs on most Canadian goods, declaring that Canada has unfairly discriminated against American autos, alcohol and dairy products.

stories.title: Victorious Spanish team parade through Madrid after World Cup triumph
stories.summary: Spain's World Cup-winning team toured Madrid on an open-top bus as huge crowds lined the streets to celebrate their victory over Argentina

stories.title: Iran vows to bomb own land as fears grow over Kuwait incursion
stories.summary: Iranian MPs called for capturing US soldiers and launching ground attacks on Kuwait and Bahrain, as discussions grew over a possible ground operation by either side and US strikes entered their 10th consecutive day.

stories.title: Watch Texas police officer pull man from burning car
stories.summary: Bodycam and dashcam footage shows a police officer rescue a man seconds before his car was engulfed in flames in Texas.

stories.title: Wildfire spreads in southeastern France as firefighters battle blaze
stories.summary: Firefighting aircrafts battled a fast-moving wildfire in southeastern France as the blaze spread across hundreds of hectares, prompting evacuations and threatening nearby communities.

stories.title: Firefighters battle major wildfire as thousands of hectares burn in central Spain
stories.summary: Firefighters battle a major wildfire in Spain's Guadalajara province as flames destroy thousands of hectares and force more than 1,200 people to flee.

stories.title: Russian prison intercepts cat used to smuggle drugs into penal colony
stories.summary: In Russia, offenders reportedly used a cat to smuggle illegal drugs into Correctional Colony No. 17 in the city of Nizhny Novgorod.

stories.title: US appeals court rejects Biden bid to block release of memoir ghostwriter recordings
stories.summary: A divided three-judge panel of the US Court of Appeals for the District of Columbia Circuit suspended its decision until 3 August to give Biden more time to consider a further appeal.

stories.title: Aliyev says former Russian and German officials were in Baku for possible meeting over Ukraine
stories.summary: Azerbaijan’s president revealed that former Russian and German officials were in Baku at the same time earlier in July, but that Azerbaijan’s authorities were neither notified nor aware of the meeting, which might have involved discussions over Ukraine.

stories.title: Chaos erupts in Buenos Aires after Argentina's World Cup final defeat
stories.summary: Riot police use water cannon in Buenos Aires after Argentina's World Cup final defeat to Spain, as protests break out in the capital.

stories.title: ‘When I get home, my whole body aches’: extreme heat saps health and wages of India’s poorest workers
stories.summary: Exclusive: Outdoor workers losing up to 9% of income as weather becomes ‘poverty multiplier’, research says Extreme heat is pushing some of India’s poorest workers further into poverty, with income losses of up to 9% for outdoor workers, new research has found. Analysis done by the International Institute for Environment and Development (IIED), and shared exclusively with the Guardian, found that extreme heat is acting as a “poverty multiplier” for hundreds of millions of workers in India’s info

stories.title: Germany news: Resistance to Nazi terror commemorated
stories.summary: Monday marks 82 years since "Operation Valkyrie," the failed attempt on Adolf Hitler's life in 1944. Meanwhile, Foreign Minister Wadephul is heading to Africa on official business.

stories.title: Meet Andy Burnham, Britain's new prime minister
stories.summary: Andy Burnham has been sworn in as the successor to British Prime Minister Keir Starmer. Burnham, the former Manchester mayor, has become a leading figure in the Labour Party and one of the UK's most popular politicians.

stories.title: UN chief Guterres to visit Cyprus in bid to revive peace talks
stories.summary: UN Secretary-General Antonio Guterres will visit Cyprus next week in what is seen as an effort to kickstart the stalled peace process. Why now? What is known about the plan? And what might be different this time round?

stories.title: AliExpress hit with record EU fine
stories.summary: The Chinese retailer has become the latest major online marketplace to face penalties under the EU’s Digital Services Act.

stories.title: US hits Iran for ninth night as Khamenei calls ceasefire 'worthless'
stories.summary: US Central Command said it targeted military command centres, air defence systems and missile sites overnight, as Iran's ayatollah called Trump's signature on the interim deal "worthless and invalid" and a negotiator said Tehran was suspending its commitments.

stories.title: Trump and Infantino hand World Cup trophy to Spain
stories.summary: Donald Trump and Gianni Infantino hand the World Cup trophy to Spain captain Rodri after Spain beat Argentina in the final near New York.

stories.title: Spain fans celebrate World Cup victory
stories.summary: Spain fans celebrate their World Cup final victory over Argentina in Times Square, New York, waving flags, chanting and lifting a replica trophy.

stories.title: Ukraine fires 400 drones at Moscow as Russia steps up ballistic missile attacks
stories.summary: Ukraine's latest attack follows a weekend of escalating exchanges between the two sides, with Russia targeting Kyiv with ballistic missiles on Sunday and Ukraine hitting warehouses outside of Moscow on Saturday.

stories.title: High divers take the plunge at Moscow's Two Rivers festival
stories.summary: High divers from 11 countries compete at Moscow's Two Rivers festival, featuring river jumps, rowing races and hydroflight performances.

stories.title: IRGC pledges 'unforgettable lesson' for US as Iran signals return to talks
stories.summary: The Revolutionary Guard told Ayatollah Khamenei it regarded his message as "a binding command," as Iran's foreign ministry said diplomacy remained open and mediators sought a 10-day ceasefire to revive the framework deal.

stories.title: Yemen’s Houthis threaten Bab el-Mandeb closure in embargo on Saudi Arabia
stories.summary: The Iran-backed Houthis declared a maritime embargo on Saudi Arabia after accusing the kingdom of attacking Sana'a airport and maintaining a years-long blockade, putting one of the world's busiest shipping lanes at risk.

stories.title: Seven Americans quarantining at Kenya Ebola facility after US travel ban, says aid group
stories.summary: Aid workers are first known people to quarantine at facility, which sparked huge opposition in Kenya Seven American aid workers who had been in Congo to fight the Ebola outbreak are quarantining at a new isolation facility in ⁠Kenya after the US government introduced travel ⁠restrictions, the head of a US charity ​employing them told Reuters. The aid workers are the first known people to quarantine at the facility, which has sparked huge opposition in Kenya and is at the heart of a legal case in

stories.title: Russian army deserters in Germany: 'No choice but to run'
stories.summary: Almost all asylum applications by Russian deserters are being denied by German authorities. DW and Russian-language media outlet Astra spoke to one of the young men who fears he'll be sent back to die on the front lines.

stories.title: The week around the world in 20 pictures
stories.summary: Israeli airstrikes in Gaza, wildfires in Europe, ICE in Maine and the World Cup semi-finals – the past seven days as captured by the world’s leading photojournalists Continue reading...
